inro: noun. A small decorative box, hung from the sash of a kimono, for holding small objects.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.

minor: adjective. Lesser, smaller in importance, size, degree, seriousness, or significance compared to another option, particularly:
minority: noun. The state or condition of being smaller, inferior, or subordinate to something or someone else; lesserness.
mint: noun. A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.
minty: adjective. Having a flavor or essence of mint.
mir: noun. A traditional village community in Imperial Russia, characterised by self-government and collectivist control of local lands.
miry: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of a mire; swampy, boggy.
mity: adjective. Alternative spelling of mitey (“infested with mites”).
mon: noun. The former currency of Japan until 1870, before the yen.
mor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Moro.

nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
nori: noun. A type of seaweed, a red alga, laver (genus Pyropia, including species P. yezoensis and P. tenera).
norm: noun. That which is normal or typical.
not: adverb. Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
omit: verb. To leave out or exclude.
ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
rim: noun. An edge around something, especially when circular.
riot: noun. A tumultuous disturbance of the public peace by a large group of people, often involving violence or damage to property.
rom: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Romani.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
roti: noun. A kind of unleavened flatbread commonly consumed in South Asia and the Caribbean.
ryot: noun. A farmer or tiller of the soil.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
tom: noun. The male of the domesticated cat, especially if not neutered.
ton: noun. Any of various units of mass, originally notionally equal to the contents of a tun, particularly: The short ton of 2000 pounds (about
tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.
torii: noun. A traditional Japanese gate at Shinto shrines, symbolically marking the transition from the profane to the sacred.
tory: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Tory.
toy: noun. Something to play with, especially as intended for use by a child.
trim: verb. To reduce slightly; to cut; especially, to remove excess.
trio: noun. A group of three people or things.
troy: adjective. Of, or relating to, troy weight.
yin: noun. A principle in Chinese and related East Asian philosophies associated with dark, cool, female, etc. elements of the natural world.
yom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yombe.
yon: determiner. Distant, but within sight; (that thing) just over there.

imi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anamgura.
imo: prepositional phrase. Alternative form of IMO.
iyo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mesaka.
mino: noun. A traditional Japanese raincoat made from straw.
miny: adjective. Of or resembling a mine.
mirin: noun. A form of Japanese rice wine, less alcoholic than saké and used in cooking.
miro: noun. A large conifer of New Zealand, Pectinopitys ferruginea (syn.
mont: noun. mount; mountain. (Can we add an example for this sense?)
morin: noun. A yellow flavonoid, extracted from fustic, once used to dye wool
mri: symbol. ISO 639-2 /T & ISO 639-3 language code for Māori.
noy: verb. To annoy; to harm or injure.
omni: noun. A person or animal who is not vegan; one with no particular dietary restrictions.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
ory: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Odia.
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
rynt: verb. stand off; move away (said by milkmaids to their cows after milking them)
